results.title: Apple Reports First Quarter Results: Revenue $124.3 Billion, EPS $2.40
results.snippet: Apple today announced financial results for its fiscal 2026 first quarter ended December 28, 2025. Revenue reached $124.3 billion, up 4% year-over-year, with iPhone revenue of $69.1 billion and Services revenue of $26.3 billion, a record high. Net income was $36.3 billion, or $2.40 per diluted share.

results.title: Apple Intelligence: AI Features Reach 700 Million Devices as Adoption Surges
results.snippet: Apple's AI platform, Apple Intelligence, has been activated on over 700 million compatible devices globally since its launch. CEO Tim Cook highlighted the integration of AI writing tools, priority notifications, and Siri enhancements as key drivers of iPhone 16 upgrade cycles. Apple plans to expand Apple Intelligence to additional languages in early 2026.

results.title: Apple Services Revenue Hits Record $26.3 Billion on App Store and Subscriptions Growth
results.snippet: Apple's Services segment, encompassing the App Store, Apple Music, iCloud, Apple TV+, and Apple Pay, generated a record $26.3 billion in Q1 FY2026, representing 21% of total revenue. The segment's operating margin exceeds 35%, making it the company's most profitable business unit. Paid subscriptions surpassed 1.1 billion across all Apple platforms.

results.title: Apple Stock Buyback Program Continues: $90 Billion Returned to Shareholders in FY2025
results.snippet: Apple returned $90 billion to shareholders through buybacks and dividends in fiscal year 2025. The company has $65 billion in net cash and continues to be the largest corporate stock repurchaser in the S&P 500. Apple's board authorized an additional $110 billion buyback programme in April 2025, the largest in company history.
